# Stoicism

Stoicism is a Hellenistic philosophy founded in Athens by Zeno of Citium around
300 BCE. Its central ethical claim is that virtue is the only true good and is
sufficient for happiness. A core doctrine is the dichotomy of control: some
things are within our power and some are not, and tranquility comes from focusing
on what we control. Prominent later Stoics include Seneca, Epictetus, and the
emperor Marcus Aurelius, whose notebook is known as the Meditations.
